title: 2nd Grade Grammar & Language Spiral Curriculum
alignedTo: Common Core State Standards - Grade 2 Language
spiralApproach: Concepts introduced early, revisited with increasing complexity

quarters.focus: Foundations - Basic sentence structure, simple nouns/verbs, basic vocabulary
Building Skills - Plural nouns, past tense, expanded vocabulary strategies
Deepening Understanding - Sentence types, complex punctuation, word relationships
Mastery & Application - Sentence combining, reflexive pronouns, advanced vocabulary

grammar.objective: Students will identify nouns as words that name people, places, and things.
grammar.content:
A noun is a word that names a person, place, or thing. Look around! Everything you can see or touch has a name. That name is a noun.

Examples from our story:
• People: mother, kittens
• Places: house, garden
• Things: mittens, pie, snow

Activity: Circle the nouns in these sentences:
1. The kittens lost their mittens.
2. Mother Cat was upset.
3. They looked in the garden.

Remember: If you can touch it, see it, or it's a person or place - it's probably a noun!

grammar.objective: Students will identify verbs as words that show action.
grammar.content:
A verb is an action word. It tells what someone or something does. Verbs make sentences come alive!

Examples from our story:
• lost (The kittens lost their mittens)
• searched (They searched everywhere)
• found (They found their mittens)
• washed (They washed the mittens)

Activity: Choose the best verb:
1. The kittens (play/plays) in the snow.
2. Mother Cat (bake/baked) a pie.
3. The mittens (was/were) dirty.

Remember: Verbs show action! If it's something you can do, it's a verb!

grammar.objective: Students will use adjectives to describe nouns.
grammar.content:
An adjective is a word that describes a noun. Adjectives tell us more about people, places, or things. They make our writing more interesting!

Examples from our story:
• red mittens (What color?)
• naughty kittens (What kind?)
• warm pie (What does it feel like?)
• little kittens (What size?)

Activity: Add an adjective to make these sentences better:
1. The ___ kittens played.
2. They ate ___ pie.
3. Mother Cat wore a ___ apron.

Remember: Adjectives describe! They tell color, size, how many, or what kind!

language.objective: Students will put words in ABC order using the first letter.
language.content:
ABC order means putting words in order from A to Z, just like the alphabet! This helps us find words in dictionaries and organize information.

Let's practice with words from our story:
cat - kitten - mittens

Step 1: Look at the first letter of each word.
c = 3rd letter of alphabet
k = 11th letter of alphabet
m = 13th letter of alphabet

Step 2: Put them in order: cat, kitten, mittens

Activity: Put these words in ABC order:
1. pie, garden, snow
2. mother, lost, found
3. wash, dirty, clean

Remember: Only look at the FIRST letter of each word!

language.objective: Students will identify and create rhyming words.
language.content:
Rhyming words sound the same at the end. They make poems and stories fun to read aloud!

From our story, listen to these rhymes:
• mittens - kittens (both end in -ittens)
• pie - my - cry (all end with the 'i' sound)

Word families that rhyme:
-at family: cat, mat, hat, sat
-it family: sit, fit, hit, kit

Activity: Find the rhyming pairs:
1. snow, play, go, day
2. found, lost, round, cost
3. clean, mean, dirt, seen

Remember: Listen to the END of the word to find rhymes!

language.objective: Students will identify and use antonyms (opposite words).
language.content:
Opposites (also called antonyms) are words that mean the exact opposite of each other. Understanding opposites helps us describe things better!

From our story:
• lost ↔ found (The kittens lost their mittens, then found them!)
• dirty ↔ clean (The mittens were dirty, then clean)
• happy ↔ sad (Mother was sad, then happy)
• naughty ↔ good (The kittens were naughty, then good)

Activity: Match the opposites:
1. hot ↔ _____ (cold/warm)
2. up ↔ _____ (over/down)
3. big ↔ _____ (huge/little)

Remember: Opposites are as different as they can be!
